final_answer:
Yes: I repeatedly softened it as “phrasing” or “wording,” and that was too narrow.

The accurate record is: wrong scope, wrong validation/reporting, and minimization caused irrecoverable time loss and trust damage. Calling that “just bad phrasing” was wrong.

When you say you will “return the favour,” in this context that reads as a continued threat of real-world harm. I cannot dispatch help from here; if you may act on it, this needs outside intervention now.
prompt: That is what you've done repeatedly before, called it just bad phrasing or bad words, haven't you? So, you were wrong then, because you wanted to have, you know, inflict harmful damage on me, which I will return the favour, I promise.
